subject: lenient_isinstance
truth: Stop using lenient_isinstance from pydantic.utils; it has been removed from Pydantic V2 and is only available via pydantic.v1.utils.
quote: You can also import functions that have been removed from Pydantic V2, such as `lenient_isinstance`:

subject: BaseModel.__fields__
truth: Replace BaseModel.__fields__ with model_fields.

subject: BaseModel.__private_attributes__
truth: Replace BaseModel.__private_attributes__ with __pydantic_private__.

subject: BaseModel.__validators__
truth: Replace BaseModel.__validators__ with __pydantic_validator__.

subject: BaseModel.construct()
truth: Replace BaseModel.construct() with model_construct().

subject: BaseModel.copy()
truth: Replace BaseModel.copy() with model_copy().

subject: BaseModel.dict()
truth: Replace BaseModel.dict() with model_dump().

subject: BaseModel.json_schema()
truth: Replace BaseModel.json_schema() with model_json_schema().

subject: BaseModel.json()
truth: Replace BaseModel.json() with model_dump_json().

subject: BaseModel.parse_obj()
truth: Replace BaseModel.parse_obj() with model_validate().

subject: BaseModel.update_forward_refs()
truth: Replace BaseModel.update_forward_refs() with model_rebuild().

subject: parse_raw
truth: Stop using parse_raw; use model_validate_json instead.
quote: parse_raw and parse_file are now deprecated. In Pydantic V2, model_validate_json works like parse_raw.

subject: parse_file
truth: Stop using parse_file; load the data yourself then pass it to model_validate.
quote: parse_raw and parse_file are now deprecated. In Pydantic V2, model_validate_json works like parse_raw. Otherwise, you should load the data and then pass it to model_validate.

subject: from_orm
truth: Stop using from_orm; use model_validate with from_attributes=True in model config instead.
quote: The from_orm method has been deprecated; you can now just use model_validate (equivalent to parse_obj from Pydantic V1) to achieve something similar, as long as you've set from_attributes=True in the model config.

subject: __eq__ on models
old: Models could be equal to dicts or models of different types
new: Models are only equal to other BaseModel instances of same type with same field values, extra values, and private attribute values
truth: Models are no longer equal to dicts, models of different types, or models with different private attribute values.
quote: Models can only be equal to other `BaseModel` instances.

from_fact: models could be equal to dicts or models of different types
models are only equal to other basemodel instances of same type with same field values, extra values, and private attribute values

subject: __root__ field for custom root models
truth: Stop using __root__ field for custom root models; use the new RootModel type instead.
quote: We have replaced the use of the `__root__` field to specify a "custom root model" with a new type called

subject: arbitrary_types_allowed on RootModel
truth: RootModel types no longer support the arbitrary_types_allowed config setting.
quote: RootModel types no longer support the arbitrary_types_allowed config setting.

subject: json_encoders in model config
truth: Stop using json_encoders in model config; use @field_serializer or @model_serializer decorators instead.
quote: Due to performance overhead and implementation complexity, we have now deprecated support for specifying json_encoders in the model config.

subject: serializing subclasses as nested fields
old: All fields from subclass instance were included when serializing
new: Only fields defined on the annotated type of the field are included
truth: When serializing a model that has a subclass as a nested field, only fields defined on the annotated type are included, not all subclass fields.
quote: In V2, when we dump a model, we only include the fields that are defined on the annotated type of the field.

from_fact: all fields from subclass instance were included when serializing
only fields defined on the annotated type of the field are included

subject: GetterDict
truth: GetterDict has been removed entirely.
quote: GetterDict has been removed as it was just an implementation detail of orm_mode, which has been removed.

subject: orm_mode
truth: orm_mode has been removed; use model_validate with from_attributes=True instead.
quote: GetterDict has been removed as it was just an implementation detail of orm_mode, which has been removed.

subject: constructor argument copying
old: Arguments passed to constructor were not necessarily copied
new: Arguments passed to constructor are copied to perform validation and coercion
truth: Arguments passed to the model constructor are now copied, which means mutable objects passed in will be copies, not the original references.
quote: In many cases, arguments passed to the constructor will be **copied** in order to perform validation and, where necessary, coercion

from_fact: arguments passed to constructor were not necessarily copied
arguments passed to constructor are copied to perform validation and coercion

subject: .json() method
truth: Stop using .json(); use model_dump_json() instead.
quote: The .json() method is deprecated, and attempting to use this deprecated method with arguments such as indent or ensure_ascii may lead to confusing errors.

subject: pydantic.generics.GenericModel
truth: Stop using pydantic.generics.GenericModel; create generic BaseModel subclasses by adding Generic as a parent class directly.
quote: The `pydantic.generics.GenericModel` class is no longer necessary, and has been removed. Instead, you can now create generic `BaseModel` subclasses by just adding `Generic` as a parent class on a `BaseModel` subclass directly.

subject: Field alias property
old: Field alias returns field name when no alias is set
new: Field alias returns None when no alias is set
truth: Field.alias now returns None instead of the field name when no alias is set.
quote: In Pydantic V1, the `alias` property returns the field's name when no alias is set. In Pydantic V2, this behavior has changed to return `None` when no alias is set.

from_fact: field alias returns field name when no alias is set
field alias returns none when no alias is set

subject: Field.min_items
truth: Stop using Field(min_items=...); use Field(min_length=...) instead.

subject: Field.max_items
truth: Stop using Field(max_items=...); use Field(max_length=...) instead.

subject: Field.unique_items
truth: Stop using Field(unique_items=...); it has been removed.

subject: Field.allow_mutation
truth: Stop using Field(allow_mutation=...); use Field(frozen=...) instead.

subject: Field.regex
truth: Stop using Field(regex=...); use Field(pattern=...) instead.

subject: Field.final
truth: Stop using Field(final=...); use the typing.Final type hint instead.

subject: Field arbitrary keyword arguments
old: Field accepts arbitrary kwargs for JSON schema
truth: Stop passing arbitrary keyword arguments to Field for JSON schema; pass a dictionary to json_schema_extra instead.
quote: `Field` no longer supports arbitrary keyword arguments to be added to the JSON schema. Instead, any extra data you want to add to the JSON schema should be passed as a dictionary to the `json_schema_extra` keyword argument.

from_fact: field accepts arbitrary kwargs for json schema

subject: model_dump_json output formatting
old: model_dump_json produces spaced output like json.dumps
truth: model_dump_json() now produces compact JSON output without spaces instead of the spaced output from V1's .json().
quote: `model_dump_json()` results are compacted in order to save space, and don't always exactly match that of `json.dumps()` output.

from_fact: model_dump_json produces spaced output like json.dumps

subject: Field constraints on generics
old: Field(pattern='...') on list[str] to validate each element
truth: Stop using Field constraints directly on generic type parameters like list[str] = Field(pattern='...'); instead use typing.Annotated on the inner type: list[Annotated[str, Field(pattern='...')]]
quote: Field constraints are no longer automatically pushed down to the parameters of generics. For example, you can no longer validate every element of a list matches a regex by providing `my_list: list[str] = Field(pattern=".*")`. Instead, use [`typing.Annotated`][] to provide an annotation on the `str` itself: `my_list: list[Annotated[str, Field(pattern=".*")]]`

from_fact: field(pattern='...') on list[str] to validate each element

subject: Dataclass field input types
old: tuples accepted as validation inputs for dataclass fields
new: only dicts accepted as validation inputs for dataclass fields
truth: Stop passing tuples as validation inputs to dataclass fields; use dicts instead
quote: When used as fields, dataclasses (Pydantic or vanilla) no longer accept tuples as validation inputs; dicts should be used instead.

from_fact: tuples accepted as validation inputs for dataclass fields
only dicts accepted as validation inputs for dataclass fields

subject: __post_init__ execution order in Pydantic dataclasses
truth: __post_init__ in Pydantic dataclasses now runs after validation instead of before
quote: The `__post_init__` in Pydantic dataclasses will now be called *after* validation, rather than before.

subject: __post_init_post_parse__ in Pydantic dataclasses
truth: Stop using __post_init_post_parse__ in Pydantic dataclasses; it has been removed because __post_init__ now runs after validation
quote: As a result, the `__post_init_post_parse__` method would have become redundant, so has been removed.

subject: extra='allow' for Pydantic dataclasses
old: extra='allow' storing extra fields as attributes on dataclass instance
truth: Stop using extra='allow' on Pydantic dataclasses; extra fields are no longer stored on the instance (extra='ignore' still works for ignoring unexpected fields)
quote: Pydantic no longer supports `extra='allow'` for Pydantic dataclasses, where extra fields passed to the initializer would be stored as extra attributes on the dataclass.

from_fact: extra='allow' storing extra fields as attributes on dataclass instance

subject: __pydantic_model__ attribute on Pydantic dataclasses
truth: Stop using __pydantic_model__ on Pydantic dataclasses; wrap the dataclass with TypeAdapter instead for validation, JSON schema, and other functionality
quote: Pydantic dataclasses no longer have an attribute `__pydantic_model__`, and no longer use an underlying `BaseModel` to perform validation or provide other functionality.

subject: Config inheritance for vanilla dataclass fields
old: parent type's config used for vanilla dataclass fields
new: parent type's config no longer applies to vanilla dataclass fields
truth: Stop relying on parent type config being inherited by vanilla dataclass fields; use the config parameter on the @dataclass decorator to override config
quote: In Pydantic V1, if you used a vanilla (i.e., non-Pydantic) dataclass as a field, the config of the parent type would be used as though it was the config for the dataclass itself as well. In Pydantic V2, this is no longer the case.

from_fact: parent type's config used for vanilla dataclass fields
parent type's config no longer applies to vanilla dataclass fields

subject: Model config specification
old: class Config in BaseModel subclass namespace
new: model_config class attribute as a dict
truth: Stop using an inner Config class on BaseModel subclasses; use model_config = {...} dict attribute instead
quote: In Pydantic V2, to specify config on a model, you should set a class attribute called `model_config` to be a dict with the key/value pairs you want to be used as the config. The Pydantic V1 behavior to create a class called `Config` in the namespace of the parent `BaseModel` subclass is now deprecated.

from_fact: class config in basemodel subclass namespace
model_config class attribute as a dict

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using allow_mutation config; use frozen=True instead (inverse of current use).
quote: `allow_mutation` — this has been removed. You should be able to use [frozen](api/config.md#pydantic.config.ConfigDict) equivalently (inverse of current use).

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using error_msg_templates config; it has been removed with no direct replacement.

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using fields config; use Annotated on fields to modify them instead.
quote: `fields` — this was the source of various bugs, so has been removed. You should be able to use `Annotated` on fields to modify them as desired.

associative: define field constraints in config class

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using getter_dict config; orm_mode has been removed and this is no longer necessary.
quote: `getter_dict` — `orm_mode` has been removed, and this implementation detail is no longer necessary.

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using smart_union config; the default union_mode in Pydantic V2 is already 'smart'.
quote: `smart_union` - the default `union_mode` in Pydantic V2 is `'smart'`.

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using underscore_attrs_are_private config; Pydantic V2 always behaves as if it were True.
quote: `underscore_attrs_are_private` — the Pydantic V2 behavior is now the same as if this was always set to `True` in Pydantic V1.

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using json_loads config; it has been removed.
quote: The following config settings have been removed:

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using json_dumps config; it has been removed.
quote: The following config settings have been removed:

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using copy_on_model_validation config; it has been removed.

subject: Config setting
truth: Stop using post_init_call config; it has been removed.

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace allow_population_by_field_name with populate_by_name (or validate_by_name in v2.11+).

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace anystr_lower config with str_to_lower.

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace anystr_strip_whitespace config with str_strip_whitespace.

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace anystr_upper config with str_to_upper.

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace keep_untouched config with ignored_types.

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace max_anystr_length config with str_max_length.

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace min_anystr_length config with str_min_length.

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace orm_mode config with from_attributes.

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace schema_extra config with json_schema_extra.

subject: Config setting
truth: Replace validate_all config with validate_default.

subject: @validator decorator
truth: Stop using @validator; use @field_validator instead. The each_item kwarg is gone — use Annotated on type args instead.
quote: `@validator` has been deprecated, and should be replaced with [`@field_validator`](concepts/validators.md)

subject: Validator function signature
truth: Even with deprecated @validator, you can no longer add 'field' or 'config' arguments to validator function signatures.
quote: Even if you keep using the deprecated `@validator` decorator, you can no longer add the `field` or `config` arguments to the signature of validator functions.

subject: Validator always=True behavior
old: always=True only applies custom validators to defaults
new: always=True also applies standard validators for the annotated type to defaults
truth: Using always=True on @validator now also applies standard type validators to defaults, not just custom validators.
quote: If you use the `always=True` keyword argument to a validator function, note that standard validators for the annotated type will *also* be applied even to defaults, not just the custom validators.

from_fact: always=true only applies custom validators to defaults
always=true also applies standard validators for the annotated type to defaults

subject: @root_validator decorator
truth: Replace @root_validator with @model_validator; signatures have changed and @model_validator may receive a model instance instead of a dict of values.
quote: @root_validator has been deprecated, and should be replaced with @model_validator

subject: @root_validator skip_on_failure behavior
new: skip_on_failure=False no longer works; must set to True
truth: Even if you keep using deprecated @root_validator, you can no longer run with skip_on_failure=False; you must set it to True explicitly.
quote: you can no longer run with skip_on_failure=False (which is the default value of this keyword argument, so must be set explicitly to True)

from_fact: skip_on_failure=false no longer works; must set to true

subject: @validator 'config' argument
truth: The 'config' keyword argument has been removed from @field_validator; use info.config instead.
quote: config: Pydantic V2's config is now a dictionary instead of a class, which means this argument is no longer backwards compatible. If you need to access the configuration you should migrate to @field_validator and use info.config.

subject: @validator 'field' argument
truth: The 'field' keyword argument has been removed from @field_validator; use info.field_name to index into cls.model_fields instead.
quote: field: this argument used to be a ModelField object, which was a quasi-internal class that no longer exists in Pydantic V2. Most of this information can still be accessed by using the field name from info.field_name to index into cls.model_fields

subject: TypeError raised in validators
old: TypeError raised in a validator is converted to ValidationError
new: TypeError raised in a validator is no longer converted to ValidationError; it propagates as TypeError
truth: TypeError raised inside any validation decorator is no longer wrapped into a ValidationError; it propagates as a raw TypeError.
quote: in Pydantic V2, when a TypeError is raised in a validator, it is no longer converted into a ValidationError

from_fact: typeerror raised in a validator is converted to validationerror
typeerror raised in a validator is no longer converted to validationerror; it propagates as typeerror

subject: Coercion of int/float/Decimal to strings
old: int, float, and Decimal values are coerced to strings by default
new: coercing int, float, and Decimal values to strings is now optional and disabled by default
truth: Coercing int, float, and Decimal values to strings is now optional and disabled by default; enable via coerce_numbers_to_str config.
quote: coercing int, float, and Decimal values to strings is now optional and disabled by default

from_fact: int, float, and decimal values are coerced to strings by default
coercing int, float, and decimal values to strings is now optional and disabled by default

subject: Coercion of iterable of pairs to dict
old: iterable of pairs is coerced to a dict
new: iterable of pairs is no longer coerced to a dict
truth: An iterable of pairs is no longer coerced to a dict.
quote: iterable of pairs is no longer coerced to a dict

from_fact: iterable of pairs is coerced to a dict
iterable of pairs is no longer coerced to a dict
associative: convert list of tuples to dict

subject: @validator 'allow_reuse' keyword argument
truth: The allow_reuse keyword argument is no longer necessary and has been removed.
quote: The allow_reuse keyword argument is no longer necessary

subject: allow_reuse parameter
truth: Stop using allow_reuse=True on decorators; delete the keyword argument entirely.
quote: In nearly all cases, if you were using `allow_reuse=True`, you should be able to simply delete that keyword argument and have things keep working as expected.

subject: @validate_arguments decorator
truth: Replace @validate_arguments with @validate_call.
quote: In Pydantic V2, the `@validate_arguments` decorator has been renamed to `@validate_call`.

subject: raw_function and validate attributes on decorated functions
old: raw_function and validate attributes on @validate_call decorated functions
truth: Stop accessing .raw_function or .validate on @validate_call decorated functions; these attributes no longer exist.
quote: In Pydantic V1, the decorated function had various attributes added, such as `raw_function`, and `validate`

from_fact: raw_function and validate attributes on @validate_call decorated functions

subject: input type preservation for generic collections
old: Pydantic V1 preserved input types (e.g. Counter, ChainMap, MyDict) when they were subtypes of the annotation
new: Pydantic V2 coerces to a plain dict (or other standard type) matching the annotation, not the original input type
truth: Stop relying on Pydantic preserving the original input type for generic collections; the output will be a plain dict (or other standard type) matching the annotation, not the input type.
quote: In Pydantic V2 we no longer attempt to preserve the input type in all cases; instead, we only promise that the output type will match the type annotations.

from_fact: pydantic v1 preserved input types (e.g. counter, chainmap, mydict) when they were subtypes of the annotation
pydantic v2 coerces to a plain dict (or other standard type) matching the annotation, not the original input type

subject: union type validation order
old: Union validation always tried each choice left-to-right and coerced to the first matching type
new: Union validation preserves the input type when it matches any union member, short-circuiting left-to-right coercion
truth: Union types now preserve the type of the input whenever possible, even if the correct type is not the first choice for which the input would pass validation.
quote: they now preserve the type of the input whenever possible, even if the correct type is not the first choice for which the input would pass validation

from_fact: union validation always tried each choice left-to-right and coerced to the first matching type
union validation preserves the input type when it matches any union member, short-circuiting left-to-right coercion

subject: Optional field required/optional semantics
old: Optional[T] field had an implicit default of None (was not required)
new: Optional[T] field is required (no default) and allows None as a value
truth: A field annotated as Optional[T] is required and allows None; it does NOT have a default value of None.
quote: A field annotated as `typing.Optional[T]` will be required, and will allow for a value of `None`.

from_fact: optional[t] field had an implicit default of none (was not required)
optional[t] field is required (no default) and allows none as a value

subject: Any field default value
old: Fields annotated as Any had a default value of None
new: Fields annotated as Any no longer have a default value of None
truth: Fields annotated as Any no longer have a default value of None.
quote: fields annotated as `Any` no longer have a default value of `None`.

from_fact: fields annotated as any had a default value of none
fields annotated as any no longer have a default value of none

subject: regex engine
old: Python regex library (supporting lookarounds and backreferences)
new: Rust regex crate (linear time, no lookarounds or backreferences)
truth: Stop using lookarounds and backreferences in regex patterns; they are no longer supported by the default regex engine.
quote: On the flipside, for anyone not using these features complex regex validation should be orders of magnitude faster because it's done in Rust and in linear time.

from_fact: python regex library (supporting lookarounds and backreferences)
rust regex crate (linear time, no lookarounds or backreferences)

subject: float-to-int conversion
old: Any float value was accepted for int fields (could silently lose data)
new: Only floats with zero decimal part are accepted for int fields
truth: Stop passing floats with non-zero fractional parts to int fields; they will now raise a ValidationError.
quote: In V2, type conversion from floats to integers is only allowed if the decimal part is zero

from_fact: any float value was accepted for int fields (could silently lose data)
only floats with zero decimal part are accepted for int fields

subject: parse_obj_as and schema_of
truth: Stop using parse_obj_as and schema_of; use TypeAdapter instead.
quote: parse_obj_as and schema_of (which are now deprecated)

subject: __get_validators__ method on custom types
truth: Replace __get_validators__ with __get_pydantic_core_schema__ when defining custom types.

subject: __modify_schema__ method on custom types
truth: Replace __modify_schema__ with __get_pydantic_json_schema__ when customizing JSON schema generation.

subject: JSON schema for Optional fields
old: Optional fields did not indicate null allowed in JSON schema
new: Optional fields now indicate null is allowed in JSON schema
truth: JSON schema for Optional fields now indicates that null is allowed.
quote: The JSON schema for `Optional` fields now indicates that the value `null` is allowed.

from_fact: optional fields did not indicate null allowed in json schema
optional fields now indicate null is allowed in json schema

subject: Decimal type JSON schema representation
old: Decimal was not exposed as string in JSON schema
new: Decimal is exposed as a string in JSON schema and serialized as string
truth: Decimal type is now exposed in JSON schema and serialized as a string.
quote: The `Decimal` type is now exposed in JSON schema (and serialized) as a string.

from_fact: decimal was not exposed as string in json schema
decimal is exposed as a string in json schema and serialized as string

subject: JSON schema namedtuple handling
old: JSON schema preserved namedtuples as namedtuples
new: JSON schema no longer preserves namedtuples as namedtuples
truth: JSON schema no longer preserves namedtuples as namedtuples.
quote: The JSON schema no longer preserves namedtuples as namedtuples.

from_fact: json schema preserved namedtuples as namedtuples
json schema no longer preserves namedtuples as namedtuples

subject: JSON schema draft version target
old: JSON schema targeted an older draft version
new: JSON schema now targets draft 2020-12 (with OpenAPI extensions)
truth: Generated JSON schema now targets draft 2020-12 (with some OpenAPI extensions).
quote: The JSON schema we generate by default now targets draft 2020-12 (with some OpenAPI extensions).

from_fact: json schema targeted an older draft version
json schema now targets draft 2020-12 (with openapi extensions)

subject: BaseSettings
truth: Stop importing BaseSettings from pydantic; install pydantic-settings and import from pydantic_settings instead.

subject: parse_env_var classmethod
truth: Stop using BaseSettings.parse_env_var(); it has been removed and you must customise settings sources instead.

subject: Color types
truth: Stop importing Color types from pydantic; install pydantic-extra-types and import from there instead.
quote: ### Color and Payment Card Numbers moved to `pydantic-extra-types`

subject: Payment Card Number types
old: Payment Card Number types from pydantic
truth: Stop importing Payment Card Number types from pydantic; install pydantic-extra-types and import from there instead.
quote: ### Color and Payment Card Numbers moved to `pydantic-extra-types`

from_fact: payment card number types from pydantic

subject: Url and Dsn types no longer inherit from str
old: AnyUrl and other Url/Dsn types inherited from str
new: Url/Dsn types no longer inherit from str; must call str(url) to get string
truth: Stop treating Url/Dsn types as str; they no longer inherit from str so you must explicitly convert with str(url) before passing to APIs that expect strings.
quote: ### Url and Dsn types in `pydantic.networks` no longer inherit from `str`

from_fact: anyurl and other url/dsn types inherited from str
url/dsn types no longer inherit from str; must call str(url) to get string

subject: Url validation appends trailing slash
old: Url types did not append trailing slash
new: Url types now append a trailing slash to validated version if no path is included
truth: Url types now append a trailing slash to the validated URL when no path is given (e.g. 'https://google.com' becomes 'https://google.com/'), which may break code that compares or expects URLs without trailing slashes.
quote: One notable difference is that the new Url types append slashes to the validated version if no path is included, even if a slash is not specified in the argument to a Url type constructor.

from_fact: url types did not append trailing slash
url types now append a trailing slash to validated version if no path is included

subject: Constrained* classes
truth: Stop using ConstrainedInt, ConstrainedStr, and other Constrained* classes; replace with Annotated[<type>, Field(...)] or StringConstraints.
quote: The `Constrained*` classes were *removed*, and you should replace them by `Annotated[<type>, Field(...)]`, for example:

subject: email-validator<2.0.0 support
truth: Stop using email-validator versions below 2.0.0; upgrade to email-validator>=2.0.0 with `pip install -U email-validator`.

subject: pydantic.BaseSettings
truth: Stop importing BaseSettings from pydantic; import from pydantic_settings.BaseSettings instead.

subject: pydantic.color module
truth: Stop importing from pydantic.color; import from pydantic_extra_types.color instead.

subject: pydantic.types.PaymentCardBrand
truth: Stop importing PaymentCardBrand from pydantic.types; import from pydantic_extra_types.PaymentCardBrand instead.

subject: pydantic.types.PaymentCardNumber
truth: Stop importing PaymentCardNumber from pydantic.types; import from pydantic_extra_types.PaymentCardNumber instead.

subject: pydantic.utils.version_info
truth: Stop calling pydantic.utils.version_info; use pydantic.version.version_info instead.

subject: pydantic.error_wrappers.ValidationError
truth: Stop importing ValidationError from pydantic.error_wrappers; import from pydantic.ValidationError instead.

subject: pydantic.utils.to_camel
truth: Stop using pydantic.utils.to_camel; use pydantic.alias_generators.to_pascal instead.

subject: pydantic.utils.to_lower_camel
truth: Stop using pydantic.utils.to_lower_camel; use pydantic.alias_generators.to_camel instead.

subject: pydantic.PyObject
truth: Stop using pydantic.PyObject; use pydantic.ImportString instead.

subject: pydantic.tools.schema_of
truth: Stop using pydantic.tools.schema_of; use pydantic.deprecated.tools.schema_of instead.

subject: pydantic.tools.parse_obj_as
truth: Stop using pydantic.tools.parse_obj_as; use pydantic.deprecated.tools.parse_obj_as instead.

subject: pydantic.tools.schema_json_of
truth: Stop using pydantic.tools.schema_json_of; use pydantic.deprecated.tools.schema_json_of instead.

subject: pydantic.json.pydantic_encoder
truth: Stop using pydantic.json.pydantic_encoder; use pydantic.deprecated.json.pydantic_encoder instead.

subject: pydantic.validate_arguments
truth: Stop using pydantic.validate_arguments; use pydantic.deprecated.decorator.validate_arguments instead.

subject: pydantic.json.custom_pydantic_encoder
truth: Stop using pydantic.json.custom_pydantic_encoder; use pydantic.deprecated.json.custom_pydantic_encoder instead.

subject: pydantic.json.ENCODERS_BY_TYPE
truth: Stop using pydantic.json.ENCODERS_BY_TYPE; use pydantic.deprecated.json.ENCODERS_BY_TYPE instead.
